titleEn: NPPC backs FAA rule to restrict drones near hog farms
titleZh: 美国猪肉生产者委员会支持FAA限制无人机靠近猪场的新规
summaryZh: 美国国家猪肉生产者委员会（NPPC）正式表态支持联邦航空管理局（FAA）一项拟议规则，该规则允许养猪场等固定设施申请限制无人机在其周边空域飞行。NPPC指出，未经授权的无人机活动对猪场的生物安全、工人安全、运营及物理安全构成严重威胁，尤其是动物权益活动人士长期利用无人机进行监视、入侵甚至破坏。该规则被视为保护关键基础设施、保障食品供应链和农业生物安全的重要一步。
translationZh:
美国国家猪肉生产者委员会（NPPC）就联邦航空管理局（FAA）一项拟议规则发表了意见。该规则旨在建立一套程序，允许FAA所称的“固定场所设施”申请限制无人机在其周边关键基础设施空域内的使用。

NPPC在评论中表示，该法规是“保护关键基础设施、保障国家食品供应、捍卫农场生物安全以及确保无人机负责任使用所必需的、且早该迈出的一步”。

NPPC的提交意见重点阐述了低空无人机活动对养猪生产带来的农场生物安全、工人安全、运营及物理安全威胁，并列举了以养猪场为目标的激进组织的已知记录。

NPPC强烈支持FAA的拟议规则，明确允许畜禽养殖场、饲料加工厂以及肉类和家禽加工设施限制未经授权的无人机在其运营区域附近飞行，并对入侵行为设定处罚。

该组织写道：“加强对符合条件的农业设施的保护，将增强农场和食品安全，保护猪群免受生物安全威胁，保障在美国养猪场工作的男女员工的安全，并强化国家食品供应的韧性——同时保留对无人机技术利益的负责任使用。”

动物权益活动人士数十年来一直以养猪场和其他畜牧场为目标。鉴于激进组织有物理入侵、监视和破坏动物农业设施的有据可查的模式，生产者担心未经授权的无人机将被用于针对他们的农场，危及动物、工人和食品供应的安全。
featuredReason: 该新闻虽不直接涉及智慧畜牧核心技术（如IoT、AI），但无人机监管与农场生物安全、自动化监控等智慧畜牧场景密切相关，对行业政策环境具有重要参考价值。

News

NPPC backs FAA rule to restrict drones near hog farms

Submission addresses farm biosecurity, worker safety, operational and physical security threats that low-altitude drone activity poses to pork operations.

National Pork Producers CouncilNational Pork Producers CouncilJuly 15, 2026

schedule1 Min Read

Getty Images

The National Pork Producers Council weighed in on a proposed Federal Aviation Administration rule establishing a process for what the FAA calls fixed-site facilities to petition for restrictions on the use of unmanned aircraft – drones – in airspace around such critical infrastructure.

In comments, NPPC said the regulation is a “necessary and overdue step toward protecting critical infrastructure, safeguarding the nation’s food supply, defending farm biosecurity,” and ensuring the responsible use of drones.

NPPC’s submission focuses on addressing the farm biosecurity, worker safety, operational and physical security threats that low-altitude drone activity poses to pork operations and on the demonstrated record of activist organizations that target hog farms.

NPPC strongly supports the FAA’s proposed rule to expressly allow livestock and poultry farms, feed operations, and meat and poultry processing facilities to restrict unauthorized drone use around their operations and to establish penalties for intrusions.

Related:Bills reintroduced to support small-scale farmers, ag workers

“Strengthening protections around eligible agricultural facilities will enhance farm and food security, protect the swine herd from biosecurity threats, safeguard the men and women who work on America’s hog farms, and reinforce the resilience of the nation’s food supply – all while preserving responsible access to the benefits of unmanned aircraft technology,” wrote the organization.

Animal rights activists have targeted swine and other livestock farms for decades. Given the documented pattern of activist organizations physically intruding on, surveilling, and damaging animal agriculture facilities, producers are concerned that unauthorized drones will be used against their farms, jeopardizing animals, workers and the safety of the food supply.
